customManagers.description: Bump the yoziru/tesla-ble git dependency pinned in idf_component.yml
Bump the ESP-IDF version used by the CI build action
Bump the esp-web-tools version pinned in the browser installer

packageRules.description: Never auto-merge a tracked firmware dependency, even when CI is green. A green `build` job only proves the firmware compiles — it does NOT prove the Tesla BLE crypto/pairing/signed-command path still works (yoziru/tesla-ble), nor that WiFi/BLE coexistence and runtime behaviour survive an ESP-IDF bump, nor that the browser installer still flashes (esp-web-tools, which the build doesn't exercise at all). These are only validated by a human running the live e2e against a real paired car. Renovate still opens, rebases and rebuilds the PR; a human reviews, runs e2e, and merges manually. (GitHub Action digest bumps are unaffected and keep auto-merging.)
Remind to re-check yoziru/tesla-ble compatibility on every ESP-IDF update (does not block the update).
prBodyNotes: ⚠️ **Before merging — verify `yoziru/tesla-ble` compatibility.** ESP-IDF 6.0 bundles Mbed TLS 4.x (PSA Crypto is primary; most legacy crypto APIs removed). tesla-ble (<= v5.1.1) does its P-256 ECDH via `mbedtls_ecp_mul` + `MBEDTLS_PRIVATE` opaque-field access, key handling via `mbedtls_pk_*`, and AES-GCM via legacy `mbedtls_gcm_*` — all removed/disallowed under Mbed TLS 4, so 6.x builds fail. Check that the CI `build` job is green; if this is a 6.x update, confirm a tesla-ble release with PSA-Crypto support exists and bump it too (`main/idf_component.yml`). Also migrate `json` -> `espressif/cjson` for IDF 6. See #61, and the decision record with the explicit start-the-shim trigger (final 12 months of IDF 5.x support with no upstream movement): docs/adr/0002-idf6-mbedtls4-crypto-seam.md.
